US market summary
Equity markets diverge as mega-cap tech selloff continues
3 sites
keyboard_arrow_up
Major U.S. indexes closed a volatile week with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq posting their fifth consecutive daily decline as of June 26, 2026. This downturn was driven largely by a sharp retreat in artificial intelligence and semiconductor stocks, which offset gains in traditional sectors like healthcare and financial services.

Jun 27, 2:44 PM
The current financial narrative in mid-2026 centers on valuation normalization and divergent growth. While AI-driven tech remains a long-term theme, the market is increasingly scrutinizing earnings quality and favorability in "Old Economy" sectors that offer stability.
Strategic Recommendations (1-3 Year Horizon)
Asset Type	Action	Reasoning
Large Cap / Growth	Accumulate	High-quality growth leaders like Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) and Qualcomm (NASDAQ:QCOM) are currently trading at P/E ratios in the low 20s, which is historically reasonable for their market dominance.
link

International ETFs	Buy/Hold	Vanguard Total International Stock ETF (NASDAQ:VXUS) remains a strong play for 2026. It captures undervalued markets outside the U.S., offering a hedge against high domestic valuations.
link

Consumer Staples	Consider Trimming	Assets like Conagra Brands (NYSE:CAG) and General Mills (NYSE:GIS) face headwinds in a narrative favoring growth; they often underperform when the focus shifts to tech productivity gains.
Crypto Stocks	Strong Hold	Per your bullish stance, Coinbase (NASDAQ:COIN) and Robinhood (NASDAQ:HOOD) are central to the digital asset ecosystem. Keep these for the full 3-year cycle.
Industrial / Transport	New Position	FedEx (NYSE:FDX) is currently trading at a P/E around 16.8x, offering a "fairly valued" entry for a 1-3 year recovery play in global logistics.
link
Summary & Insights
Focus for 2026: High-confidence growth stocks with sustainable margins (e.g., MSFT at 39% net profit margin) are prioritized over speculative names.
link
Portfolio Cleanup: Consider dropping lagging consumer brands (KHC, GIS, CAG) that have limited growth catalysts, and reallocate that capital into "fairly valued" tech like Salesforce (NYSE:CRM) or semiconductor leaders.
link
International Diversification: Keep adding to VXUS or VWO as emerging market cycles typically turn faster than mature markets in mid-decade recoveries.
Calculated using Google Finance data. Might be shortened for brevity.
link
